concerns.text: FR-006 mandates 'CPU-only environment' and 'no GPU acceleration', but the Assumptions section states the system will use 'image generation (using a lightweight Stable Diffusion XL model)'. Generating SDXL images on CPU is computationally prohibitive for a study requiring 20+ images per participant (potentially 3000+ total images) and contradicts the 'Compute feasibility' assumption which claims it will 'fit within the RAM and CPU time limits of the GitHub Actions free tier'. This creates a conflict between the functional requirement for CPU-only execution and the feasibility of the stated methodology.

The Edge Cases section states: 'The system must exclude their partial data... or impute only if a specific missing-data strategy is defined (currently excluded).' This phrasing is ambiguous. It implies the strategy is 'currently excluded' (i.e., not defined), yet the requirement says 'impute only if... defined'. If the strategy is undefined, the 'or impute' branch is impossible, making the 'exclude' branch the only valid path. The text should be clarified to explicitly state that partial data is excluded, removing the conditional 'or impute' clause to avoid confusion about whether imputation is a valid fallback.
